strengths: Three specific real-world objects are clearly identified and contextualized (JWST, piano, tennis ball).
Use of concrete examples (Federer for tennis) and empirical support (UC Berkeley study) grounds the analysis in real contexts.
The IA prompt is clearly stated, giving the exhibition a focused foundation.
weaknesses: Commentary remains largely descriptive and does not consistently engage with TOK concepts (ways of knowing, claims/counterclaims).
Links between objects and the prompt lack depth and precision, drifting into personal narrative rather than analytical discussion.
Evidence is uneven: some assertions (e.g. JWST objectives) lack support, and references to TOK terminology are minimal.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exhibition meets the basic requirements of the 5â6 band by clearly identifying three objects with real-world contexts and attempting links to the prompt âWhy do we seek knowledge?â Strengths include specific examples and some empirical support. However, the analysis remains descriptive and superficial, with limited explicit engagement with TOK concepts and uneven evidence. These factors place the work at the lower end of the 5â6 band, justifying a final score of 5.

annotations.comment: The Federer example grounds the analysis in a recognized real-world context of professional tennis.
Consider analysing competition as a way of knowing, exploring how challenge and feedback generate knowledge.
The IA prompt is clearly stated, providing a focused foundation for the exhibition.
The word count exceeds the 950-word limit for the TOK exhibition, risking non-compliance with IB guidelines.
Consider clarifying how the title and introduction engage with the knowledge question rather than only restating the prompt.
The Sound of Life citation lacks publication date and author details; refine to meet academic conventions.
The opening paragraph is largely descriptive and does not engage with TOK concepts such as ways of knowing or knowledge questions.
Frame a precise knowledge question early on (e.g., âHow does curiosity drive technological exploration?â) to guide your analysis.
Object 1 is clearly identified as the James Webb Space Telescope with a specific real-world referent.
The description of JWSTâs objectives is broad but lacks explicit linkage to the prompt beyond general curiosity.
Provide citations or data to support claims like âexamines every phase of cosmic historyâ to strengthen evidence.
The final sentence on this page trails off, indicating a missing conclusion to the thought on human curiosity.
Stating that JWST may have âlittle to no practical useâ needs justification in relation to knowledge value, ideally contrasting practical vs. theoretical pursuits.
Object 2 (the piano) is clearly stated, grounding the analysis in a specific real-world object.
Inclusion of the UC Berkeley study provides empirical support for emotional reactions to music.
The link between learning piano and seeking knowledge is descriptive; deepen the analysis by considering how emotion serves as a way of knowing.
Engage with specific ways of knowing (e.g., emotion) and link them explicitly to the prompt in your justification.
The reflection on feelings is personal but lacks connection to a broader knowledge question or counter-claim.
Introduce a knowledge question such as âTo what extent does emotional understanding constitute knowledge?â to frame personal reflections.
Object 3 is crisply identified as a personal tennis ball, situating the exhibition in the studentâs lived experience.
The discussion of tennis remains at a descriptive level and doesnât explain how competition relates to seeking knowledge in a TOK sense.
Despite using a strong example, the commentary remains descriptive and doesnât engage with TOK language of claims, counter-claims, or WOKs.
Add a brief conclusion linking all three objects back to the prompt and reflecting on overarching knowledge implications.
QuillBot summarizer is not a recognized scholarly source; replace with peer-reviewed or primary academic references.
The NASA citation lacks a retrieval date. Include access dates for web sources to meet IB citation standards.
Ensure all in-text claims (e.g., cost of JWST, emotional impacts) are matched with citations listed here.

strands.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
